message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should exit non-zero and write to stderr when more damage entries of a type are reported than were insured"
Expected: runScenario throws when number of damage entries of a type exceeds count of insured items of that type
Current error: returns silently (processes both damages without check)
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

In processClaim, count damages by itemType and count policy items by type. For each itemType in damages, if (damage count > policy item count), throw Error("too many damages of type <itemType>: N reported, only M insured"). 

Be careful: test 35 (two swords damaged) has 2 sword damages with 2 swords insured — that's allowed. Only throw when damage count strictly exceeds insured count for that type.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal code to make this failing test pass.

content.text:
All 39 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added a check in `processClaim` that counts damages by `itemType` and counts insured items by type. If damage count for a type strictly exceeds insured count for that type, throws an error with the message `too many damages of type <itemType>: N reported, only M insured`.
- Result: All tests now pass (39 passing, including the new failing test and the existing "two damaged swords" test where 2 damages == 2 insured swords is allowed).
- Approach: Minimal — just builds two count maps and checks the strict inequality. No optimization or refactoring; existing logic preserved.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-15_05-48-04_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
